message:
feat(fnp-python): native fromstring for text-sep parsing (br-franken_numpy-hsw8)

Replace the pure np.fromstring passthrough with a Rust text-token parser
that handles the non-deprecated `sep=<non-empty>` path end-to-end:

- Accept str or bytes (bytes decoded as UTF-8; fallback for non-UTF-8
  bytes payloads since numpy decodes via latin-1/ASCII at the C layer).
- If sep is all-whitespace, split by whitespace runs (`str::split_whitespace`).
  Otherwise split on the exact sep, trim each token, drop empties.
- Apply count: -1 means "consume all"; negative-other-than-1 returns
  empty; positive N truncates.
- Parse each token per requested dtype. Integer dtypes accept both
  integer-literal tokens and integer-valued float literals (matching
  numpy's tolerance for `"1.0"` in int32 mode). Floats route through
  `str::parse::<f64>` and downcast. Bool accepts int and float forms.
- Any parse failure (malformed token, non-numeric text, etc.) falls
  back to numpy so the ValueError message surface stays aligned.

Empty sep still delegates: numpy emits a DeprecationWarning (and in
modern releases a ValueError) for the binary-interpretation path, and
our wrapper shouldn't independently reproduce that deprecation warning.

Test fromstring_matches_numpy_across_sep_whitespace_dtype_count_and_errors
passes on rch; full suite: 395/395 fnp-python lib tests green.
